creator: sf@maxempire.com
text:
I do not approve this patch as I believe it to not be necessary (also, it 
has a few mistakes, probably made by bad search/replace operation).

In case of this port, rsync should basically behave like cp(1) and not 
use -p option as suggested in the patch.

The real problem lies in rsync and some recent change to the ZFS file 
system, as stated in the following thread on freebsd-fs mailing list: 
http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-fs/2010-March/007945.html

So, I'm guessing that it would be best to submit a separate bug report 
against rsync in order to properly fix the problem.

--
Regards,
Marko Njezic - mr.maX @ MAX Interactive corp.

> Maintainer of security/clamav-unofficial-sigs,
>
> Please note that PR ports/144639 has just been submitted.
>
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
> and a committer will take care of it.
>
> The full text of the PR can be found at:
>    http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/144639
>
> -- 
> Edwin Groothuis via the GNATS Auto Assign Tool
> edwin@FreeBSD.org
